data.title: Australia unemployment rate rises to 4.1% in January
data.description:
• Australia’s unemployment rate edged higher to 4.1% in January 2025, up from December’s 4.0%, meeting estimates.

  • The participation rate rose to a record peak of 67.3%, surpassing forecasts of 67.1%. The underemployment rate remained at 6.0%.

  • “The rises in both the number of people employed and unemployed saw the participation rate rise by 0.1 percentage point, to a new record high of 67.3 per cent. This was 0.8 percentage points higher than a year ago and 1.8 percentage points higher than March 2020." commented Bjorn Jarvis, ABS head of labour statistics.

  • ETFs with interest linked to Australia: (EWA), (FXA), (FLAU).

    Currency: (AUD:USD) (USD:AUD)

data.title: Thursday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM Jobless Claims

New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time. The four-week moving average of new claims smooths out weekly volatility.

Claims are expected to rise to 215,000 in the latest week after falling by 7,000 to 213,000 a week earlier. The four-week moving average last week was down 1,000 at 216,000.

8:30 AM Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index

The general conditions index from this business outlook survey is a diffusion index of manufacturing conditions within the Philadelphia Federal Reserve district.

The Philly index caught everyone off guard with an amazing jump to 44.3 in January from minus 10.9 in December. The call for February looks for still-strong growth at 22.7.

9:35 AM Fed's Goolsbee Speech

Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee will participate in a moderated question-and-answer session before the Chicagoland Chamber Mid-Market Chicago event.

10:00 AM Leading Indicators

The index of leading economic indicators is a composite of 10 forward-looking components including building permits, new factory orders, and unemployment claims. The report attempts to predict general economic conditions six months out.Forecasters see the leading index flat in January after a 0.1 percent decline in December.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on natural gas stocks in underground storage for the U.S. and five regions of the country. The level of inventories helps determine prices for natural gas products.

11:00 AM EIA Petroleum Status Report

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on petroleum inventories in the U.S., whether produced here or abroad. The level of inventories helps determine prices for petroleum products.

12:05 PM Fed's Musalem Speech

St. Louis Federal Reserve Bank President Alberto Musalem will speak and participate in moderated conversation on the U.S. economy and monetary policy before the Economic Club of New York.

2:30 PM Fed's Barr Speech

Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michael Barr will speak on"Supervision and Regulation" at the Georgetown University Law Center.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed's balance sheet is a weekly report presenting a consolidated balance sheet for all 12 Reserve Banks that lists factors supplying reserves into the banking system and factors absorbing reserves from the system.

5:00 PM Fed's Kugler Speech

Federal Reserve Board Governor Adriana Kugler will speak on"Navigating Inflation Waves While Riding on the Phillips Curve" before the 2025 Whittington Lecture event hosted by Georgetown University.

data.title: Glencore signals $1B buyback and $1.2B shareholder distribution in 2025 amid strong EBITDA performance
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Glencore plc (OTCPK:GLCNF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Gary Nagle highlighted a strong 2024 performance with adjusted EBITDA reaching $14.4 billion. The industrial business delivered $10.6 billion, driven by a robust metals segment, despite weaker energy contributions due to lower prices. Marketing EBIT reached the upper end of the $2.2 billion to $3.2 billion guidance range.
  • The company announced a $1.2 billion cash distribution to shareholders and a $1 billion buyback program, set for completion by August 2025. Nagle noted the buyback signals Glencore's return to active stock repurchase after a year-long hiatus due to the $7 billion EVR acquisition.
  • CFO Steven Kalmin emphasized the balance sheet strength with net debt at $11.2 billion, translating to a net debt-to-adjusted EBITDA ratio below 0.8. Kalmin noted the potential for additional distributions or buybacks mid-year.
  • Nagle highlighted the successful integration of the EVR steelmaking coal business, which contributed $1 billion to EBITDA in its first six months under Glencore ownership. Cost savings of 14% and productivity improvements were achieved in the second half of 2024.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated its focus on disciplined capital allocation and supply restraint, particularly in copper, where investments in major projects like Coroccohuayco, MARA, and El Pachon will align with market conditions. Nagle stated the company would prioritize bringing these projects online when prices and deficits justify the investment.
  • Glencore plans to maintain its disciplined approach to coal production and may announce supply cuts in Q1 to address market oversupply, primarily in high-quality coal segments.
  • The company expects continued strong performance from its marketing business, supported by volatility in commodity markets and arbitrage opportunities.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted EBITDA was $14.4 billion, reflecting a 16% decline mainly due to reduced energy prices. The industrial business contributed $10.6 billion, while marketing EBIT reached $3.2 billion.
  • Steelmaking coal margins were strong, with Canadian assets leading the cost performance. Zinc operations delivered $1.4 billion in EBITDA, with improvements in cost efficiency and production volumes.
  • The EVR acquisition added $1 billion in EBITDA during the second half of 2024, with further synergies expected in 2025.
  • Operating cash flow after tax and interest reached $10.5 billion, driven by strong H2 performance. Net cash capital expenditure totaled $6.7 billion, including $700 million for EVR.

Q&A

  • Izak Rossouw, Barclays: Questioned the viability of underperforming assets like African copper and Australian zinc. CEO Nagle emphasized supply discipline and highlighted the long-term value of high-grade copper assets, noting that Mutanda's ramp-up would wait for improved market conditions.
  • Jason Fairclough, Bank of America: Asked about M&A opportunities and synergies with Teck Resources. Nagle confirmed openness to value-accretive deals but noted slow progress on synergies with Teck.
  • Liam Fitzpatrick, Deutsche Bank: Inquired about the timeline for potentially shifting Glencore's primary listing to the U.S. Nagle confirmed active consideration but no definitive timeline.
  • Myles Allsop, UBS: Asked about coal pricing and market dynamics. Nagle expressed optimism for steelmaking coal, citing limited supply expansion and strong demand growth, particularly in India.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed skepticism about the viability of underperforming assets and the timeline for project execution. Concerns about market oversupply in coal were prominent, though optimism about steelmaking coal pricing was noted.
  • Management maintained a confident tone overall, emphasizing the company's ability to generate cash and return value to shareholders. Nagle repeatedly underscored Glencore’s supply discipline and strategic flexibility.
  • Compared to prepared remarks, the tone during Q&A was more defensive, particularly on asset performance and M&A discussions.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language emphasized disciplined capital deployment and supply management, consistent with prior statements.
  • Analysts focused on synergies, potential asset sales, and M&A, reflecting heightened interest in portfolio optimization and shareholder returns.
  • Management tone was confident but more cautious regarding market uncertainties and geopolitical risks.

Risks and Concerns

  • Market oversupply in high-quality coal segments may pressure prices. Management indicated plans for potential production cuts to stabilize the market.
  • Weak cobalt prices and underperformance in certain assets, such as African copper operations, remain challenges.
  • Geopolitical uncertainties and tariff impacts pose risks to industrial and marketing activities.

Final Takeaway

Glencore delivered strong financial results in 2024, supported by a robust metals business and disciplined supply management. The company's return to shareholder distributions, including a $1 billion buyback, underscores confidence in its cash generation capabilities. While challenges remain in certain assets and market segments, Glencore's focus on capital discipline, supply restraint, and strategic flexibility positions it well for sustained performance. The successful integration of EVR and ongoing project developments in copper signal potential for future growth, subject to market conditions.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Rogers Corporation targets gradual recovery in EV/HEV markets with new production facility in China
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Rogers Corporation (NYSE:ROG) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Colin Gouveia highlighted that Q4 results were in line with expectations, with sales, gross margins, and adjusted earnings near guidance midpoints. The quarter faced challenges from market headwinds, particularly in the EV/HEV and industrial markets, which experienced inventory destocking and reduced demand.
  • The company reported significant progress on operational cost reductions and innovation initiatives in 2024, including securing key design wins across major categories such as EV/HEV, portable electronics, and aerospace and defense.
  • Laura Russell was officially appointed as CFO during the quarter. CEO Gouveia emphasized her financial expertise, particularly within the semiconductor segment, as a key asset for the company’s strategic growth.
  • CFO Laura Russell stated, "Sales of $192 million, gross margin of 32.1%, and adjusted EPS of $0.46 were all near the mid-point of our previously announced guidance." She also underscored the focus on managing operational costs and generating solid free cash flow.

Outlook

  • Management guided Q1 2025 revenues to be between $180 million and $195 million, with gross margins expected in the range of 29% to 30.5%. Adjusted EPS is forecasted between $0.10 and $0.40, reflecting restructuring-related expenses.
  • CEO Gouveia noted that Q1 would likely represent a low point for 2025, with a gradual recovery anticipated in the second half, particularly in the EV/HEV and industrial markets. He cited a cautious customer environment driven by trade policy uncertainties and inventory management.
  • New production capacity in China is expected to contribute to growth in the second half of 2025, with curamik factory qualifications concluding mid-year.

Financial Results

  • Q4 revenues were $192 million, a 9% sequential decline, attributed to lower portable electronics and wireless infrastructure sales. Gross margin for the quarter was 32.1%, down 300 basis points from Q3, driven by lower volume and unfavorable product mix.
  • Adjusted EPS was $0.46 for Q4, compared to $0.98 in Q3, due to higher operating expenses and reduced gross margins.
  • Full-year 2024 sales reached $830 million, a 9% decline from the prior year. However, operational cost-saving measures limited gross margin contraction to 40 basis points year-over-year.
  • Free cash flow for 2024 totaled $71 million, supported by effective cost controls and working capital management.

Q&A

  • Jeremy, CJS Securities: Asked about the sequential revenue decline in Q1 and potential recovery in the second half. CEO Gouveia highlighted portable electronics seasonality and curamik business challenges but reiterated expectations for stronger second-half performance due to customer recovery and new production capacities in China.
  • Unidentified Analyst, B. Riley Securities: Asked about growth trajectory in aerospace and defense. CEO Gouveia characterized the segment as a mid-single-digit growth business long-term, supported by strong program wins and diversified applications.
  • Craig Ellis, B. Riley Securities: Asked about the incremental impact of the new curamik facility in China. CEO Gouveia emphasized the facility’s role in supporting silicon carbide power modules, noting that much of the production would represent new business rather than a shift in existing operations.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on recovery timelines and strategic growth initiatives. Questions were largely neutral to slightly pressing, reflecting concerns about inventory challenges and customer hesitancy.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing operational excellence and innovation. However, responses in the Q&A revealed a more cautious stance on near-term market challenges, with phrases like "gradual recovery" and "uncertainty related to trade policies" indicating tempered expectations.
  • Compared to Q3, management’s tone reflected slightly less confidence in immediate demand recovery, though strategic initiatives were highlighted as key long-term growth drivers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 guidance was met, but revenues and margins declined sequentially. Key drivers included lower portable electronics and wireless infrastructure sales, compared to a stronger Q3 boosted by seasonal demand.
  • While Q3 emphasized a ribbon-cutting for the curamik factory in China, Q4 detailed its operational timeline, signaling mid-2025 contributions.
  • Analysts in Q4 probed deeper into market-specific recovery timelines, particularly for EV/HEV and industrial segments, reflecting heightened concerns versus broader Q3 inquiries.
  • Management maintained a consistent focus on cost-saving initiatives and strategic investments but flagged additional geopolitical and trade policy uncertainties in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Industrial and EV/HEV markets remain under pressure due to inventory destocking and reduced demand. Management flagged that recovery in these segments would be gradual, with significant improvement not expected until the second half of 2025.
  • Geopolitical uncertainties, including trade policy and tariffs, were cited as ongoing risks. Management expressed confidence in the local-for-local manufacturing strategy as a mitigating factor.
  • Portable electronics demand is subject to seasonal fluctuations and delayed customer adoption of new technologies, posing short-term headwinds.

Final Takeaway

Rogers Corporation delivered Q4 results in line with expectations, supported by operational cost controls and strategic investments. While the company faces near-term challenges in key markets like EV/HEV and industrial, management anticipates gradual recovery in the second half of 2025. The new curamik facility in China and ongoing innovation initiatives are positioned to drive long-term growth, with targeted improvements in customer demand and operational efficiencies expected to materialize later in the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: ACV Auctions targets $785M 2025 revenue with 150% adjusted EBITDA growth amid AI advancements
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ACV Auctions Inc. (NASDAQ:ACVA) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO George Chamoun emphasized the company’s performance in Q4 2024, highlighting that revenue and adjusted EBITDA exceeded the high end of guidance. Full-year revenue grew by 32%, driven by strong execution in the dealer wholesale business and growth in ACV Transport and ACV Capital.
  • Over 50% of U.S. dealers are now transacting on the ACV marketplace, reinforcing significant market share gains.
  • ACV Capital achieved full-year revenue growth of 26%, with new offerings like dealer financing for consumer-sourced vehicles showing early traction.
  • The company continues to invest in AI-powered tools, including ACV MAX, which predicts vehicle prices with high accuracy, enhancing marketplace efficiency.
  • CFO William Zerella noted Q4 revenue of $160 million, representing a 35% year-over-year growth, along with adjusted EBITDA of $6 million, exceeding expectations and reflecting a 900 basis point margin improvement year-over-year.

Outlook

  • For Q1 2025, revenue is projected between $180 million and $185 million, with adjusted EBITDA expected in the range of $9 million to $11 million.
  • Full-year 2025 guidance includes revenue between $765 million and $785 million, reflecting growth of 20% to 23%, and adjusted EBITDA of $65 million to $75 million, signaling approximately 150% year-over-year growth.
  • Management assumes dealer wholesale volumes to remain flat year-over-year while expecting revenue growth to outpace operating expense growth by 500 basis points.
  • Updated midterm targets include revenue of $1.4 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $350 million, supported by sustained market share gains and higher auction and assurance ARPU.

Financial Results

  • Q4 adjusted EBITDA margin increased by 900 basis points year-over-year, reflecting the strength of high-margin auction and assurance revenues.
  • Auction and assurance revenue accounted for 58% of total revenue, growing 40% year-over-year. Marketplace services revenue grew 31%, supported by ACV Transport’s 30% growth.
  • SaaS and data services contributed 5% of total revenue, with growth accelerating to 10% year-over-year.
  • ACV reported a strong capital structure with $270 million in cash and $123 million of debt at year-end, supported by $65 million in operating cash flow for the full year.

Q&A

  • Eric Sheridan, Goldman Sachs: Inquired about market share gains and assumptions embedded in forward forecasts, as well as the balance between achieving EBITDA targets and reinvesting in the business. CEO Chamoun responded that share gains are expected to continue at a consistent rate, with potential for faster growth through value-added services.
  • Chris Pierce, Needham & Company: Asked about the flat dealer wholesale volume outlook for 2025, given strong January performance. Chamoun explained mixed market signals and emphasized a prudent approach to guidance, incorporating a range to account for uncertainties.
  • Pete Lukas, CJS Securities: Queried the advantages of ACV’s reconditioning network expansion. Chamoun highlighted its role in expanding total addressable market and efficiency improvements through proprietary technology.
  • Rajat Gupta, JPMorgan: Sought updates on ACV MAX and ClearCar adoption. Chamoun noted increasing traction and partnerships with large dealer groups, with the solutions driving wholesale commitments.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on market share growth and guidance prudence amid uncertain wholesale market conditions. Key concerns included flat wholesale volumes and competitive pressures.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing revenue growth and EBITDA expansion. During Q&A, the tone shifted slightly defensive when addressing market uncertainties, as evidenced by phrases like “we think it’s prudent” and “assume right down the middle.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management appeared slightly more cautious, reflecting mixed market signals and macroeconomic headwinds.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth decelerated from 44% in Q3 to 35% in Q4, attributed to seasonality and flat dealer wholesale volumes.
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin improvement in Q4 (900 basis points) was consistent with Q3’s expansion (1,000 basis points), driven by operational efficiencies.
  • Analysts shifted focus from Q3’s optimism about market recovery to Q4 concerns about flat wholesale volumes in 2025.
  • Management’s tone remained consistent, though slightly more defensive in Q4 as they balanced growth and caution in guidance.

Risks and Concerns

  • Flat dealer wholesale volumes projected for 2025 could limit growth opportunities.
  • Continued headwinds in the used-vehicle market, including affordability challenges and inventory levels 25% below historical norms.
  • Competitive pressures from players like CarMax and Carvana, particularly in tech-driven offerings.
  • Macroeconomic uncertainties, including interest rates and potential tariffs, add complexity to market recovery projections.

Final Takeaway

ACV Auctions closed 2024 with strong revenue and adjusted EBITDA growth, driven by market share gains, value-added services, and AI-powered innovations. While cautious guidance reflects flat dealer wholesale volume expectations for 2025, the company’s investments in technology and expanded service offerings position it well for sustained long-term growth. Updated midterm targets of $1.4 billion in revenue and $350 million in adjusted EBITDA signal confidence in scaling operations and capturing market opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: IMAX targets $1.2B global box office in 2025 with expanded content slate
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: IMAX Corporation (NYSE:IMAX) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Richard Gelfond highlighted the company's momentum driven by a record-breaking Chinese New Year slate, which generated over $120 million in box office revenue. He noted that 2025 is projected to be a landmark year with an anticipated global box office of over $1.2 billion, supported by an expanded content portfolio and strategic partnerships.
  • Gelfond emphasized the company's diversification efforts, including a record 118 films released in 2024, with 61 local language films from eight countries. He also discussed new partnerships with Netflix for Greta Gerwig’s "Narnia" and Apple for an exclusive release of the F1 movie.
  • Gelfond pointed to the relaunch of IMAX’s documentary strategy, highlighted by "The Blue Angels," and further event programming such as the Paris Olympics and League of Legends championships.
  • CFO Natasha Fernandes reported adjusted EBITDA of $37 million for Q4, up 48% year-over-year, and adjusted EPS of $0.27. Full-year revenues reached $352 million, with a 39% adjusted EBITDA margin, meeting the high end of their guidance. She also noted improvements in cash flow and working capital, with operating cash flow increasing 21% year-over-year to $71 million.

Outlook

  • Management projected a record global box office of over $1.2 billion in 2025, driven by a robust slate of films, including "Avatar 3," "Mission Impossible: The Final Reckoning," and "Fantastic Four."
  • The company expects to install 145 to 160 systems in 2025, with a continued focus on joint revenue-sharing agreements, particularly in high-performing markets such as Southeast Asia, the Middle East, and South America.
  • Fernandes stated that adjusted EBITDA margins are expected to be at least 40% in 2025, supported by operating leverage as box office revenues grow.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 revenues were $93 million, up 8% year-over-year. Content Solutions revenues grew 34% to $26 million, driven by strong box office results from titles like "Interstellar."
  • Technology Products and Services revenues totaled $64 million, reflecting a 2% increase year-over-year. Gross margin for the quarter was 52%, slightly higher than the prior year.
  • Full-year 2024 system installations reached 146, with 57% under joint revenue-sharing arrangements. New system signings included 130 agreements, with 60% in growth markets.
  • Operating cash flow reached $71 million for the year, and the company maintained a strong balance sheet with $101 million in cash and $418 million in available liquidity.

Q&A

  • Eric Handler, Roth Capital: Asked about geographic growth opportunities. CEO Gelfond cited strong demand in Japan, Western Europe, and North America, with Japan already securing eight system signings in 2025.
  • Mike Hickey, The Benchmark Company: Inquired about the Netflix partnership for "Narnia." Gelfond explained that the deal required balancing the interests of studios, exhibitors, and filmmakers and noted its potential as a model for future collaborations.
  • Omar Mejias, Wells Fargo: Asked about Chinese market recovery and slate-driven growth. CEO of IMAX China Daniel Manwaring highlighted that 2025 will feature the most Film for IMAX Chinese titles ever, with big-budget local productions.
  • David Joyce, Seaport Research Partners: Inquired about adjusted EBITDA margin outlook. Fernandes emphasized a mix of cost efficiencies and operating leverage stemming from higher box office projections.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed positive sentiment, focusing on growth opportunities in China and the strength of the 2025 slate. Questions emphasized strategic partnerships and system installations, signaling optimism for sustainable growth.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, particularly regarding the Chinese market rebound and the 2025 box office outlook. Fernandes highlighted operational efficiencies and financial discipline as drivers of margin expansion.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 adjusted EBITDA of $37 million marked a 48% increase year-over-year, compared to $39 million in Q3. Adjusted EPS grew to $0.27 from $0.26 in Q3.
  • Revenues rose to $93 million in Q4, compared to $91.5 million in Q3, driven by stronger box office results in the holiday period.
  • Management tone was more confident in Q4, with significant emphasis on the Chinese market recovery and strategic partnerships with major studios and streamers.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged the challenges posed by the 2024 Chinese box office softness but expressed optimism for 2025 due to an improved slate and government support.
  • Analysts raised concerns about system renewal timelines and potential delays in installations. Management highlighted proactive measures to negotiate renewals and optimize system performance.
  • Economic uncertainties in key markets like China and Europe remain a potential risk, though mitigated by a diversified content portfolio and geographic expansion.

Final Takeaway

IMAX Corporation is poised for significant growth in 2025, with a projected global box office of over $1.2 billion, reflecting strong momentum in content expansion, system installations, and strategic partnerships. The rebound in the Chinese market, coupled with a robust slate of films and diversified revenue streams, positions the company to achieve record-breaking results while maintaining operational efficiency and financial discipline.
